prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene were computed in the gas phase using the semi-empirical GFN2-xTB Hamiltonian (xtb v6.x backend).  The calculation evaluated neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1) ferrocene, as well as its doublet cation (charge +1) and doublet anion (charge −1) to obtain f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic) and dual-descriptor values from Mulliken charges.

Geometry provenance: user-supplied experimental geometry file ‘ferrocene.xyz’; no further optimization was performed (single-point charge analysis).

Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).

Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) parent; doublet states (±1, 2) for N ± 1 species chosen automatically by the skill.

Most electrophilic (largest f⁺) atom: Fe (atom 11, index 10) with f⁺ = 0.22885456.
Most nucleophilic (largest f⁻) atom: Fe (atom 11) with f⁻ = 0.25938191.

Live run log: the xtb SCF and charge-analysis output was streamed during execution to a `.out` file in the current working directory; the results JSON is saved as ‘ferrocene_fukui_xtb.json’.

Warnings reported by chemkit (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Convergence: all SCF calculations converged; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true.

Headline value reported (f⁺ on the iron atom): 0.22885456
